title: tulipa australis var. fragrans
intro: Tulipa australis var. fragrans, also known as the Fragrant Wild Tulip, is a perennial plant species belonging to the lily family (Liliaceae) and native to the mountainous regions of eastern Australia. The plant's flowers, leaves, and roots are used in traditional medicine, and it is known to possess antimicrobial, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Tulipa australis var. fragrans include its ability to relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce pain and inflammation. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions when used under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
